storyInfo: Catherine's old friend, Clara, asks her to help her mutual aid association, but it seems that Clara has more than just the association on her mind... and Catherine can help in more ways than one.

attributes.content: Catherine? Catherine!
...Clara? It's so early... did you need something?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: *sigh* You're always so busy. I was worried I wouldn't find you at home... and I wouldn't have, if I didn't come so early.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Not that busy. I'm retired. I'm just going out to buy food for the week.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: How about you? How's your mutual aid association going?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Well, that is what I came for...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Do you think you could... help me?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Can I come with you to the grocer? It won't take long.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Alright, come and help me pick a new detergent. The one I'm using right now leaves behind too much limescale.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: You're still using Old Hank's? They changed their formula. It's cheaper, but nowhere as effective as it used to be...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: We can talk about this once we're at the shop. What's this about the mutual aid association? What do you need me for?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Well... you know that we are all from single-parent families, and we help babysit each other's children.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: It started out as a bunch of people sharing their schedules, so that they could leave their kids with someone when they have long shifts or a night shift.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: That's how we've been getting by for all these years...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I know. It's been quite a few years. I'm amazed at how you've kept it going, and taking care of all those kids.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: *sigh* The mutual aid association helped a lot with John. I was just returning the favor...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: But... there are a lot more single-parent families after the war.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: The association has to take care of a lot more kids. Nobody's home could fit them all, if we still did things the old way.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Some houses don't even have enough chairs, never mind desks for them to do their homework.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: So we were thinking about pooling our resources to rent a bigger place.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Do you have enough money?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: *sigh* We have more people, after all. We can come up with a fair amount when everyone chips in a little...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: But... well... Ian and I were looking for a place together, but we... we had a fight.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: He hasn't showed up at the association for four days...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Just let it go, Ian... there's nothing we can do if he doesn't want to rent.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: We'll keep looking...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: That bloody jerk! It's just a cafeteria warehouse! I asked around and I know for a fact his factory's not operating. What kind of 'other uses' could he have?
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: We may not have signed a contract yet, but we already paid our deposit...
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: How could he just go back on his word like that?
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: He did give us our deposit back...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: That's what I'm angry about. He just throw the bag of money at you without so much as an apology. The way he looked down at us...
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: He was the one who broke his word. No explanation, not even so much as a polite word.
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: He thinks we're people he can just walk over, doesn't he?
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: ...Do you think he's trying to drive up the price?
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: I don't think so. He would have said something if that was the case, wouldn't he?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I bet he wanted us to ask. Whoever asks first is at a disadvantage. That's how it is with bargaining.
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: *sigh* You really wanted this place, didn't you?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Of course I did. After all, you...
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: ...Never mind. What I mean is, it's the right size and location. It took us such a long time to find it, and to get a deal done...
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: It's his place, and he gets to make the decisions... we have to accept that, Ian.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Why do you keep defending that guy? Aren't we supposed to be on the same side?
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: O-Of course we are, but there's nothing we can do about it...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: What are two old folks like us supposed to do about it? If you keep whining, it'll just make me feel worse.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Alright, sorry, I didn't mean to upset you.
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: See you around.
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: ...Ian hasn't shown up at the association since.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I... I shouldn't have said what I said. Ian was just standing up for me...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I'm just too meek... I didn't dare to speak up against the factory owner. All I did was sigh and lament. Of course he would be angry.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Ian sometimes looks hesitant when he's talking to me. Has he been tired of my attitude for a long time?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Why couldn't I be like you...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: ...You don't need to be like me, Clara. Lindy wouldn't have been so angry with me if I had half your gentleness.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: At least, she would have been happier. I think.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: See, there comes a time for everyone when they're not happy with themselves.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Catherine...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: But that's enough for that. So, do you want me to help you take care of this matter?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: No, I just...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Can you come with me to meet with the factory owner again... and have my back?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Ian was right. If he wanted to haggle, well, we can talk about it. At least he wouldn't demand an exorbitant price if he sees you...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Every factory owner in Highbury knows you, after all.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: If I can get the deal done, then I can apologize to Ian...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: How do you plan to pay for the extra expense?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: I... I can pay it out of my own pocket, if it's not too much...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I-Is that alright? I hope I'm not troubling you too much...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Of course I'll help. And don't ever talk about troubling me. No need to treat me as a war hero. I'm just the same Catherine you've known for so long, the one who couldn't knit a sweater to save her life.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Let's see what's going on.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: What are you doing here? I gave you back your deposit. I don't owe you a pence now.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: What... what if we're willing to pay more for the rent?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Do you think you could, er, think about it...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Do I look like I'm after that little bit of rent you're paying?
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: Wh-What's your price? We can talk about it...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Are you deaf, grandma?
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: If I'm not renting, I'm not renting. I'm being nice because of your age. You should be grateful.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: You call that being nice?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: What's that, another grandm—wait, y-you're that war hero?
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: I'm Catherine Fletcher.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: ...Fine. What do you want?
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: I'm not planning to reopen the factory. No workers for you to rally. There's nothing you can do to me.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: Besides, this is my property, and I get to decide what to do with it. I didn't even sign a contract with her. She's not a tenant, and there's no law that can do anything about it.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: I'm showing plenty of good faith by giving her back the deposit.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: First, explain what you mean when you say you're not planning to reopen the factory.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Oh, where's this going? That 'social responsibility' stuff?
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: Me and my rotten luck. No good deed ever goes unpunished in this city.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: Sounds like you have a lot on your hands. Maybe we can find some common ground, in which case I might be able to help.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: ...Why do you want to help me?
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: Why shouldn't I, if helping you helps the workers?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Alright, at least you're not one of those bucket-heads who only know how to yell.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: But don't bother.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: You want to talk social responsibility, come back later and talk to the Columbians.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: You sold the factory to the Columbians?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Not the whole factory, just the buildings. The factory is bankrupt.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: Just because we won the war doesn't mean people like me got access to any loans.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: If you can sell the buildings, you can put it up for collateral. I know a thing or two about these things.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: No, you don't. If the Columbians didn't buy my buildings, they would buy somebody else's. At the prices they set, I wouldn't even be able to pay the interest if I tried to compete with them.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: I hung in there for two years... I was happy when we won the war. I thought I had made it.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: I could have hung on for a few more months... or so I thought. But then the Columbians came, and I didn't need to anymore.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: Unless you thought I could hire workers at half-wage, 'leader of the workers'?
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: No. Parliament wouldn't allow it.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Thanks to that Minimum Wage Bill that you people passed in Parliament...
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: For you, going bankrupt means selling your factory for a nice little sum that would let you live comfortably in retirement at your country manner.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Do you know what it means for the workers if their wages get slashed in half?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: I do, whether you believe it or not.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: I failed because I knew these things.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: Or you were just unlucky.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: The workers need not, and cannot, take responsibility for your bad luck.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: ...I hate talking to people like you.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: I don't hate talking to you. You're hardly the worst person I've met.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: I hope luck will favor the people of this city.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: I asked Clara what price you set. Like you said, you didn't rent her the warehouse for the money.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: ...It's not like I didn't want to do some good. If heaven would let me.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: But heaven didn't show as much kindness to me as I tried to showed others.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: Bah, forget it. Why am I even telling you this.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: Anyway, the warehouse's sold. You heard me? Don't come back.
attributes.name: Factory Owner

attributes.content: 'It's not that I don't want to help, I just can't help anymore.' Is that what you think?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: If that's really the case, maybe you can hear me out. There's still a way for you to gain some appreciation, if you're up for it.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: A few days later...
I'm done over here, Catherine!
attributes.name: Tall Worker

attributes.content: What? No way you worked faster than me—
attributes.name: Short-sighted Worker

attributes.content: You bet I did! I want my pint of beer.
attributes.name: Tall Worker

attributes.content: Good work, folks. Drinks are on me.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: No! We had a bet. He owes me one, even if you're buying.
attributes.name: Tall Worker

attributes.content: This round's still on me. You can collect from him later.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Please, allow me. Come over to my place afterwards, Bernie, Galvin and Catherine, and let me play host.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: No need to be so polite, Clara. It's just a little prefab, easily done.
attributes.name: Tall Worker

attributes.content: Yeah, spend that money on food for the kids, not beer for Bernie.
attributes.name: Short-sighted Worker

attributes.content: I'll let you settle that bet by turning the beer you owe me to bread for Clara.
attributes.name: Tall Worker

attributes.content: But you've helped so much...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: They're on the night shift today, Clara. They can't come.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Oh... alright. Another day, then.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: What about you, Catherine? You have the time, don't you? This could never have happened without you...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I couldn't have imagined that the factory owner was a good man... I hardly dared breathe when the two of you were talking the other day.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Good man, huh... I suppose.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: You won't find an empty plot of land in Highbury, outside of scattered spaces on factory premises. Fortunately, all he sold were the buildings, not the land.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Will we be alright when the Columbians come?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: No problem. I had a look at the place that day. There wasn't much use for this little corner anyway, other than storing some junk.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: We'll patch up that brick wall later so that it'll be separated from the factory area.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Besides, we have a proper lease signed this time. Today's Londinium has some rules, at least. There's nothing to be afraid of.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: It's all thanks to you, Catherine...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: All I've done is help out for a couple of days. It's nothing compared to how you've been taking care of the kids for so long.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: It's not like I'm good for much else at my age... it's not so much that I'm taking care of the kids as the kids are keeping me company.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Oh dear, look at me, being pessimistic again. You're not offended, are you?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: No, I'm not. I know that's the kind of person you are. Whatever you think about yourself, Clara, I think you're amazing.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: To Catherine's surprise, Clara looks sad at the answer, as though it brought back unpleasant memories.
Are you thinking about Ian? Unhappy that he's cross with you?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: ...Maybe. I can tell that sometimes when he looked at me, there's something he wanted to say, but couldn't say out loud.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: If you can tell what he's thinking, he can tell what you're thinking. Perhaps he has his own considerations.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: That day... I was quite sure there was something he wanted to say to me, but he never actually said it. Then he just got angry with me for days. What was I supposed to do...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I know, I shouldn't have said that he made me feel worse...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I want to invite everyone from the association to gather once I've cleaned up the place... do you think Ian will come?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Don't ask me. Ask him.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Day of the gathering
Can I plant something here, Clara? My teacher wants us to observe how seeds sprout. I brought the peas and cans!
attributes.name: Mischievous Child

attributes.content: Don't tell me you're trying to make canned peas sprout.
attributes.name: Composed Child

attributes.content: Don't be silly! I'm not stupid. My dad bought fresh peas from the grocery store!
attributes.name: Mischievous Child

attributes.content: Can I plant them, Clara? Can I?
attributes.name: Mischievous Child

attributes.content: Of course, darling. There's some dirt piled up over there that you can use.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Do you take care of all these children by yourself?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: There aren't so many usually. They came because of the gathering...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I'm not by myself. The others take turns to come over and help. I don't have to go to work, so I have more time.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Ian's retired too. He can help you.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Is he not here?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: ...I went to his place a couple of days ago to tell him about the gathering, but he wasn't at home.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I left a message with his neighbor, but...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Is he avoiding me?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: ...Have you ever thought about talking to him, Clara?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Talk... about what?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Forming a family, the two of you.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Wh-What are you talking about?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I'm not saying you have to form a family, but at least let him know that you like him. Don't keep everything in your heart.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: C-Catherine, I... I... you...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Did you think I couldn't tell?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: But... at my age...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: What good is there in hiding it? What do you have to lose by saying it?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: But people don't talk about these things at this age...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: What about 'this age'? It's not like your brain's stop working, or your heart's stop beating. You like being with him, care about him, and look all sullen when you don't see him. It's obvious, isn't it?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: People will gossip so much...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: And what about it? All the things that you and Ian have done at the association, what kind of person you are, it's there for everyone to see.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Taking care of children is a good thing. This is different. It's so... shameful...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I, I don't mean to say you're wrong, Catherine. I'm just... I'm talking about myself...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Are you saying that you're being 'shameful'?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: I know you. You always treat people sincerely, as you treat Ian.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: There's nothing shameful about being sincere.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: I know we're at 'this age', but all the more reason to go for what you want, while you can.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: How much time do we have left? What is there to worry about?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: But... he's still angry with me... what do I do...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: You know what kind of man Ian is, don't you? Kind, gentle soul, just like you. Did you really think he would stay angry with you?
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Even if it's not today...
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Oh, look who's here.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: *huff*... *huff*...
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: Ian!
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: You... you came?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Why are you in such a hurry...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Baldi didn't tell me until this morning! He was grinning from ear to ear, saying, 'I almost forgot! Clara came to look for you again...'
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: That bloody bastard!
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: I thought you were angry with me...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I... yes, I was a little angry that day.
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: I... never mind.
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: Don't 'never mind'. Clara gets anxious when you swallow your words like that, like she's been these few days.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: I... I wanted to have the association here because... because Clara is the one who cares for the children the most.
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: The other places were all too far from her home...
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: How much time would it take for her to go back and forth with her bad legs? What if she has an accident?
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: I... I was concerned for her, only to be told that I was being upsetting...
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: I was a little hurt.
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: Oh... Ian...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I'm so sorry, Ian! You know I always say the wrong things... I didn't really mean it...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: I know. I stopped being angry the next day.
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: Then why didn't you come to the association?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: ...I was out all day these few days, trying to come up with the money and see if I could negotiate with the factory owner...
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: Then I learned from Baldi this morning that you and Catherine had taken care of it!
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: He didn't dare play games when he saw Catherine, did he?
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: Depends on how you define 'playing games'.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: By the way, I think Clara has something to say to you.
attributes.name: Catherine

attributes.content: Oh...
attributes.name: Clara & Ian

attributes.content: Clara...?
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: Clara subconsciously clutches the edge of her jacket.
In that moment, it is as though the clock has been turned back forty years.
That day, Clara had clutched the edge of her white skirt in the same way, stuttering as she said the three magical words.
There are wrinkles all over her face now, but the expression upon it is no different from the young girl in the white dress forty years ago.
Clara opens her mouth, but no words come out.
It feels as though the air has become dense... then she hears Clara's voice.
...Ian.
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Catherine said to me... no, Catherine didn't put me to this. I wanted to say it myself...
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Ian... what do you say that we... got together?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: We... what?
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: See, you've helped me so much, and I think... I think you're really nice. What do you think about me... about you and me?
attributes.name: Clara

attributes.content: Clara... good heavens...
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: How... how could I have made you say it... I've always... I know that you... you would have minded...
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: I... heavens, Clara, I never thought... never thought you would...
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: O-Of course! I think you're really fine, really!
attributes.name: Ian

attributes.content: They say no more, their hands holding each other's tightly.
Catherine turns her head in the direction of the racket that the children are making.
The sky is overcast, but it seems to have gotten brighter than when it was an hour ago.
But of course, it is morning.
And the sun is rising.
